diagrams.title: Z-Buffer Algorithm Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the z-buffer depth comparison process where each pixel stores depth value and the closest object wins
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes how z-buffer resolves visibility by comparing depth values - the closest object (smallest Z) is rendered for each pixel

diagrams.title: Painter's Algorithm Back-to-Front Sorting
diagrams.description: Demonstrates sorting polygons from farthest to nearest and rendering in that order so closer objects overwrite farther ones
diagrams.purpose: Shows how painter's algorithm handles visibility by sorting all polygons by depth before rendering - simpler but can fail with cyclic depth relationships

diagrams.title: 3D Rendering Pipeline Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows the complete graphics pipeline from vertex input to framebuffer output with key processing stages
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the modern GPU pipeline architecture - data flows through programmable and fixed-function stages to produce rendered images

diagrams.title: BSP Tree Visibility Determination
diagrams.description: Shows binary space partitioning tree structure and how it enables back-to-front or front-to-back traversal for visibility
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how BSP trees handle static scene visibility by recursively dividing space with planes, enabling efficient depth ordering

visuals.title: Basic Implementation Strategies
visuals.description: Overview of computer graphics rendering strategies including z-buffer, painter's algorithm, and pipeline stages
